meta.title: Convert MP4 to GIF — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Turn an .mp4 video into an animated .gif right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Convert MP4 to GIF — Free, No Upload
og.description: Make an animated GIF from an .mp4, locally in your browser.
hero.title: Convert MP4 to GIF — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop a short .mp4 and turn it into an animated .gif for chats, reactions and docs. It's made on your own device, so the clip is never uploaded.
prose.p1: GIFs loop automatically, play inline everywhere, and need no player — perfect for reactions, short demos and how-to snippets in chats, issues and docs. Keep clips to a few seconds: GIFs have no real audio and get large fast.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your clip to a server first. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the GIF is built locally in your browser, so nothing is stored or logged. Pick a width and frame rate to balance smoothness against file size.
faq.a1: No. The GIF is created entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — watch your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q2: Why is my GIF so big?
faq.a2: GIF is an old format that stores frames inefficiently, so even short clips can be several megabytes. Use a smaller width and a lower frame rate to bring the size down.
faq.q3: How long can the clip be?
faq.a3: Technically any length, but GIFs balloon quickly — a few seconds is ideal. For anything longer, an MP4 or WebM will look better and weigh far less.
